req.title: Overdraft protection (negative variant)
req.statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: overdraft linked

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates overdraft protection via a linked savings account. Four key decision branches exist: (1) savings has sufficient funds -> draw succeeds + fee; (2) savings has insufficient funds -> transaction declined; (3) debit exactly equals savings balance (boundary); (4) no valid auth token (NEG/security). State transitions move the account from 'checking_insufficient' through 'overdraft_triggered' to either 'overdraft_covered' or 'declined'. Equivalence partitions: sufficient-savings vs insufficient-savings vs exact-match. Boundary: debit amount == savings available balance (on-point). Decision table covers the auth x balance matrix.

tests.name: test_overdraft_draws_from_savings_and_assesses_fee
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $10.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $500.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Valid auth token available
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=150.00 against the checking account
trace: R-FIN-062: fee assessed on overdraft draw

tests.name: test_overdraft_declined_when_savings_insufficient
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $5.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $20.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Valid auth token available
trace: R-FIN-062: transaction shall be declined when linked balance insufficient

tests.name: test_overdraft_exactly_exhausts_savings_balance
preconditions: Customer has a checking account with balance $0.00
Customer has a linked savings account with balance $100.00
Overdraft protection is enabled and linked
Valid auth token available
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=100.00 (exactly equals savings balance, on-point boundary)
trace: R-FIN-062: draw up to linked account's available balance
R-FIN-062: BVA on-point where shortfall equals exact savings balance

tests.name: test_overdraft_rejected_without_valid_auth
preconditions: A checking account with overdraft linked exists
No valid auth token supplied (unauthorized actor)
actions: POST /overdraft with a debit_amount=50.00 using an invalid/expired Bearer token
expected: No debit applied to any account
trace: R-FIN-062: actors must be authenticated customers

tests.name: test_overdraft_state_transitions_checking_to_covered
preconditions: Customer checking account is in state 'active' with balance $30.00
Customer savings account is in state 'active' with balance $300.00
Overdraft protection enabled; state machine starts at 'checking_sufficient'
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=80.00 to trigger transition: checking_sufficient -> checking_insufficient -> overdraft_triggered -> overdraft_covered
trace: R-FIN-062: triggers - debit exceeds checking balance initiates state transition
R-FIN-062: expected outcome - linked savings debited, fee assessed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:8080"

@pytest.fixture
def auth_headers():
    return <get_auth_headers_fixture>

@pytest.fixture
def overdraft_stc_account(auth_headers):
    return <setup_account_fixture>(
        checking_balance=30.00,
        savings_balance=300.00,
        overdraft_linked=True,
        auth_headers=auth_headers
    )

def test_overdraft_state_transitions_checking_to_covered(auth_headers, overdraft_stc_account):
    account_id = overdraft_stc_account["account_id"]
    payload = {"account_id": account_id, "debit_amount": 80.00}

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/overdraft",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "approved"
    assert body["savings_debited"] == pytest.approx(50.00, abs=0.01)
    assert body["overdraft_fee"] > 0

    state_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/account/{account_id}/state",
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert state_resp.status_code == 200
    assert state_resp.json()["account_state"] == "overdraft_covered"
    <teardown_account_fixture>(account_id, auth_headers)

tests.name: test_overdraft_decision_table_no_linked_account
preconditions: Customer checking account balance $5.00
No savings account linked to the checking account
Valid auth token available
actions: POST /overdraft with debit_amount=50.00 where no savings account is linked
trace: R-FIN-062: overdraft linked precondition violated -> decline path
R-FIN-062: DT rule - linked=false, balance=any -> declined
